calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2023 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-06-2023 Continued To Apr-12-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-10-2023 Continued To Feb-21-23 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 611

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Feb-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Evan Smith, Esq. of Brodsky & Smith LLC, Tel. 877.534.2590, appearing via CourtCall for the Plaintiff;
Ann Grimaldi, Esq. of Grimaldi Law Office, Tel. 415.937.8680, appearing via Zoom for the Defendant Starskin USA, Inc.

Plainitiff Anthony Ferreiro's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

The Court inquired about the matter being set directly in Department 611. Ms. Grimaldi stated that the motion was to be rescheduled to Department 611. Mr. Smith stated that the prior tentative ruling in Department 302 said that he was to refile and set it in Department 611.

Brief discussions are held. No argument is held.

Discussions conclude. Matter submitted. The Court notes that it has the proposed order and consent judgment for review.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present.

***After the hearing, the Court signed the order and consent judgment. See order and judgment filed this date.***

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Continued To Sep-28-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-12-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ANTHONY FERREIRO's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion is denied without prejudice. Any further motion to approve settlement must be noticed before Judge Karnow in Dept. 611 by scheduling it with the clerk of that Department. The current motion has these issues:

-Consent judgment [CJ] 1.4 refers to Alameda county;
-It is unclear why the court is presented with two consent judgments when only 1 is offered for signature;
-CJ 3.4: How large is the font (in order to evaluate conspicuousness)? A life-size exemplar may also suffice to demonstrate conspicuousness;
-Id. On the web site, the word "Warning" alone is not sufficient, even though it is a hyperlink. At least the full abbreviated print warning should be required on the site;
-There is no foundation for the requested attorney fees. The documents refer to a Smith declaration 14-18 but there are no declarations with those paragraphs. There is a Smith Declaration of 19 July with paragraphs only up to 8. A different Smith Declaration has paragraphs up to 11 and then switches to a part of a memorandum of points and authorities.

If the court's approval is required for the dismissal with prejudice of Macy's, there is no explanation why the dismissal should be with prejudice. I note that Macy's is expressly called out as securing the benefit in the releases applicable to Starskin, but contributes nothing to the settlement. Smith Decl. of 24th June 2022, 2. There should be an explanation why the dismissal is not without prejudice.

Counsel to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/CK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-24-2022 Continued To Aug-17-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
